output.bugFixed: Wrapper visibility on desktop - all tab wrappers were visible instead of only the active one
output.rootCause: The .tabs-item-wrapper elements had md:flex-1 but no md:hidden class, so all wrappers were visible and taking up space on desktop. The show/hide logic (md:hidden md:open:block) was only on the <details> element, which didn't control wrapper visibility.

changes: Added md:hidden to wrapper classes by default
Wrapper classes changed from 'tabs-item-wrapper md:flex-1 md:rounded-3xl transition-colors' to 'tabs-item-wrapper md:hidden md:flex-1 md:rounded-3xl transition-colors'
Added md:hidden to wrapper classes by default to mirror PHP
Wrapper classes changed from 'tabs-item-wrapper md:flex-1 md:rounded-3xl transition-colors' to 'tabs-item-wrapper md:hidden md:flex-1 md:rounded-3xl transition-colors'
Updated selectTab() to show/hide wrappers based on active state
For active wrapper: remove 'md:hidden', add 'md:flex' and 'bg-accent-lighter'
For inactive wrappers: add 'md:hidden', remove 'md:flex' and 'bg-accent-lighter'
Updated setMobileMode() to remove md:hidden and md:flex classes from all wrappers so they display as accordion items on mobile

implementation.approach: Hide all wrappers by default on desktop (md:hidden), then JavaScript shows only the active wrapper by removing md:hidden and adding md:flex

desktopBehavior.default: All wrappers have md:hidden class (not visible)
desktopBehavior.activeTab: JavaScript removes md:hidden and adds md:flex to active wrapper, adds bg-accent-lighter for background color
desktopBehavior.inactiveTabs: JavaScript adds md:hidden back and removes md:flex and bg-accent-lighter

mobileBehavior.default: md:hidden only applies at md breakpoint and above, so wrappers are visible on mobile by default
mobileBehavior.onModeSwitch: JavaScript removes md:hidden, md:flex, and bg-accent-lighter from all wrappers to ensure clean accordion display

warnings: Asset size limit warnings (expected - editor.js 337 KiB, screen entrypoint 374 KiB)

syncStatus.notes: TSX and PHP both have md:hidden added to wrapper classes. JavaScript properly manages visibility by toggling md:hidden and md:flex classes.

testingNotes: Desktop: Verify only the active tab wrapper is visible (others should be hidden)
Desktop: Verify switching tabs shows the new active wrapper and hides the previous one
Desktop: Verify active wrapper has bg-accent-lighter background
Desktop: Verify active wrapper stretches full height (md:flex-1 still applies when visible)
Mobile: Verify all wrappers are visible as accordion items
Mobile: Verify no leftover desktop classes (md:hidden, md:flex) interfering with mobile display
Resize: Switch from desktop to mobile - verify all wrappers become visible
Resize: Switch from mobile to desktop - verify only active wrapper is visible
